docs.title: AlphaFoldFetch
docs.text:
AlphaFoldFetch is a command-line tool for downloading AlphaFold structure files from UniProt IDs and UniProt FASTA files.

It is meant for fast batch workflows:

  • download one or many structure in one command
  • choose any combination of PDB, CIF, or gzipped outputs
  • tune concurrency for larger downloads

docs.title: CLI Summary
docs.text:
affetch [OPTIONS] UNIPROT...

Arguments:

  • UNIPROT...: UniProt IDs, FASTA files, or - for stdin

Options:

  • --output, -o: output directory
  • --file-type, -f: any combination of p, c, and z
  • --model, -m: AlphaFold model version 1 through 6
  • --n-sync: concurrent download requests
  • --n-save: file writes submitted per batch

docs.title: Input Rules
docs.text:
AlphaFoldFetch accepts:

  • raw UniProt accessions like P11388
  • text that contains a valid UniProt accession
  • FASTA files ending in .fasta, .fas, .fa, or .faa
  • - to read whitespace-separated input from stdin

FASTA parsing only keeps validated UniProt IDs from header lines.

docs.title: Output Rules
docs.text:
The default file-type value is pcz, which means:

  • p: PDB output
  • c: CIF output
  • z: gzip compression

So the default behavior downloads both PDB and CIF files as gzip outputs.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
Single AlphaFold structure

affetch P11388

Multiple AlphaFold structures

affetch P11388 Q01320 P41516

Structures from a single UniProt FASTA file

affetch UP000005640_9606.fasta

Multiple UniProt FASTA files

affetch UP000007305_4577.fasta UP000005640_9606.fasta UP000000625_83333.fasta
First obtain these FASTA files from UniProt

Multiple custom FASTA files

affetch plant_pgks.fasta mammalian_pgks.fasta bacterial_pgks.fasta
Input files must be in the UniProt FASTA file format

Unzipped PDB file

affetch -f p P11388
Default will dowload zipped PDB and CIF files for all entries

Redirect output to a directory

mkdir human_top2a && affetch -o ./human_top2a P11388

EBI AlphaFold Download data - Search Results CSV File

tail -n +2 results-csv.csv | while IFS='-' read -r f1 f2 f3; do echo $f2; done | affetch -

Don't know the UniProt ID? Use getSequence and pipe into affetch

getseq human top2a, mouse top2a, rat top2a | affetch -
Pipe input arguments must be indicated with a dash -

docs.title: Contributors
docs.text: • Edgar Manriquez-Sandoval

docs.title: Version 1.0.0
docs.text:
• Switched the project to a uv-native workflow with uv_build, dependency groups, and a refreshed lockfile.
  • Modernized pyproject.toml metadata and tool configuration for the current package layout.
  • Refactored the CLI implementation with stronger typing, centralized constants, and a canonical alphafold_file_url helper while keeping backward compatibility for the older misspelled helper name.
  • Added explicit typing exports in affetch.__init__ and affetch.__version__.
  • Replaced the placeholder test module with executable smoke tests for core parsing and file-writing behavior.
  • Rewrote the README, contributing guide, and MkDocs site to focus on real install, usage, development, and reference workflows.

docs.title: Version 0.0.1
docs.text: • First release

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
Thanks for helping improve AlphaFoldFetch.

This project is small and CLI-focused, so the most helpful contributions are usually one of these:

  • bug fixes
  • better tests
  • clearer docs
  • usability improvements to the CLI

docs.title: Before You Start
docs.text:
For non-trivial changes, open an issue first so the implementation direction is clear before you spend time on it.

Use the issue tracker for:

  • bug reports
  • feature requests
  • docs problems
  • behavior questions

When reporting a bug, include:

  • your OS
  • your Python version
  • the command you ran
  • a small reproducible example
  • the error output, if there is one

docs.title: Local Setup
docs.text:
Clone the repository and install dependencies with uv:

git clone git@github.com:YourLogin/alphafoldfetch.git
cd alphafoldfetch
uv sync
uv run pre-commit install

docs.title: Development Workflow
docs.text:
Create a branch for your work:

git switch --create my-change

Useful local commands:

uv run pytest
uv run ruff check .
uv run ruff format --check .
uv run mypy affetch tests
uv run affetch --help

If you change the docs, you can preview them locally:

uv run --group docs mkdocs serve --config-file mkdocs.yml --dev-addr localhost:8000

Build the docs in strict mode:

uv run --group docs mkdocs build --config-file mkdocs.yml --clean --strict

docs.title: Pull Requests
docs.text:
Before opening a pull request:

  • keep the change focused
  • add or update tests when behavior changes
  • update docs when the CLI behavior changes
  • run the local checks listed above

Then commit and push your branch:

git add <files>
git commit
git push --set-upstream origin my-change

Open a pull request from your fork and include a short explanation of:

  • what changed
  • why it changed
  • how you tested it

docs.title: Docs Contributions
docs.text: Docs changes are welcome on their own. If the improvement is clear and self-contained, a documentation-only pull request is completely fine.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Install project dependencies with uv:

uv sync

Install pre-commit hooks once:

uv run pre-commit install

docs.title: Main Commands
docs.text:
Run tests:

uv run pytest

Run lint checks:

uv run ruff check .
uv run ruff format --check .

Run type checks:

uv run mypy affetch tests

Inspect the CLI:

uv run affetch --help

docs.title: Docs Workflow
docs.text:
Serve docs locally:

uv run --group docs mkdocs serve --config-file mkdocs.yml --dev-addr localhost:8000

Build docs in strict mode:

uv run --group docs mkdocs build --config-file mkdocs.yml --clean --strict

docs.title: Project Layout
docs.text:
• affetch/: package source
  • tests/: test suite
  • docs/: documentation site
  • pyproject.toml: package metadata and tooling configuration

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Run it without installing:

uvx --from AlphaFoldFetch affetch P11388

docs.title: Install The CLI
docs.text:
Install AlphaFoldFetch with uv:

uv tool install AlphaFoldFetch

This installs the affetch command globally.

docs.title: Verify
docs.text:
affetch --help

Or:

uvx --from AlphaFoldFetch affetch --help

docs.title: Development Setup
docs.text:
For local project work:

uv sync
uv run pre-commit install

docs.title: NSave: Final = typer.Option(500, help='Concurrent number of file writes, lower value = slower write speed') module-attribute
NSync: Final = typer.Option(50, help='Syncronized number of downloads, lower value = slower download speed') module-attribute
Output: Final = typer.Option(Path('.'), '--output', '-o', help='Output directory', dir_okay=True, exists=True) module-attribute
Uniprot: Final = typer.Argument(..., help='UniProt ID(s) or FASTA file(s)', allow_dash=True) module-attribute
app = typer.Typer(name=CLI_NAME, help='A tool for downloading AlphaFold structures using UniProt IDs or FASTA files') module-attribute
affetch(uniprot: list[str] = Uniprot, *, output: Path = Output, file_type: str = FileType, model: int = Model, n_sync: int = NSync, n_save: int = NSave)
docs.text:
Source code in affetch/main.py 
@app.command()
def affetch(
    uniprot: list[str] = Uniprot,
    *,
    output: Path = Output,
    file_type: str = FileType,
    model: int = Model,
    n_sync: int = NSync,
    n_save: int = NSave,
):

    if not any(True for s in "pc" if s in file_type):
        typer.echo("No valid tile type options entered: --file-type must contain `p` and/or `c`", err=True)
        sys.exit(1)

    if model not in SUPPORTED_MODELS:
        typer.echo(f"No valid model options entered: --model must be one of {SUPPORTED_MODEL_TEXT}", err=True)
        sys.exit(1)

    if not output.exists():
        typer.echo(f"No directory named `{output.resolve()}` found", err=True)
        sys.exit(1)

    uniprot_ids: list[str] = []
    if uniprot == ["-"]:
        uniprot = [u.strip() for u in sys.stdin.read().split()]

    for u in uniprot:
        if any(u.endswith(s) for s in FASTA_SUFFIX):
            # If the input is a file, parse it, validate it, and save it
            uniprot_ids += validate_fasta(u)
            continue

        _u = u.upper()
        if validate_uniprot_id(_u):
            # If the input is a string, validate it, and save it
            uniprot_ids.append(_u)
        else:
            # Otherwise, parse it, then validate and save it
            _u = parse_uniprot(_u)
            if validate_uniprot_id(_u):
                uniprot_ids.append(_u)

    if not len(uniprot_ids):
        typer.echo("No valid UniProt IDs entered", err=True)
        sys.exit(1)

    uniprot_urls: Iterable[str] = ()
    if "p" in file_type:
        pdb_url_func = functools.partial(alphafold_file_url, model=model, file="pdb")
        pdb_urls = map(pdb_url_func, uniprot_ids)
        uniprot_urls = itertools.chain(uniprot_urls, pdb_urls)

    if "c" in file_type:
        cif_url_func = functools.partial(alphafold_file_url, model=model, file="cif")
        cif_urls = map(cif_url_func, uniprot_ids)
        uniprot_urls = itertools.chain(uniprot_urls, cif_urls)

    write_func: StructureWriter = write_structure
    if "z" in file_type:
        write_func = write_gz_structure

    for urls in chunk_urls(list(uniprot_urls), n_save):
        structures = asyncio.run(alphafold_api(urls, n_sync))

        with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor() as executor:
            for url, structure in structures.items():
                file_name = Path(url).name
                file_path = output.joinpath(file_name)
                executor.submit(write_func, file_path, structure)

docs.title: alphafold_api(urls: list[str], n_sync: int) -> dict[str, str] async
docs.text:
Source code in affetch/main.py 
async def alphafold_api(
        urls: list[str],
        n_sync: int
) -> dict[str, str]:
    sem = asyncio.Semaphore(n_sync)
    results: ResultMap = {}
    await alphafold_api_call(sem, urls, results)
    return results

docs.title: alphafold_api_call(sem: asyncio.Semaphore, urls: list[str], results: ResultMap) -> None async
docs.text:
Source code in affetch/main.py 
async def alphafold_api_call(
        sem: asyncio.Semaphore,
        urls: list[str],
        results: ResultMap
) -> None:
    async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
        tasks = [alphafold_api_coroutine(session, sem, url, results) for url in urls]
        await asyncio.gather(*tasks)

docs.title: alphafold_api_coroutine(session: aiohttp.ClientSession, sem: asyncio.Semaphore, url: str, results: ResultMap) -> None async
docs.text:
Source code in affetch/main.py 
async def alphafold_api_coroutine(
        session: aiohttp.ClientSession,
        sem: asyncio.Semaphore,
        url: str,
        results: ResultMap
) -> None:
    async with sem, session.get(url) as response:
        if response.status == http.HTTPStatus.OK:
            results[url] = await response.text()

docs.title: alphafold_api_url(uniprot: UniProtId) -> AlphaFoldUrl
docs.text:
Create AlphaFold API URLs

Source code in affetch/main.py 
def alphafold_api_url(uniprot: UniProtId) -> AlphaFoldUrl:
    """ Create AlphaFold API URLs """
    return f"{ALPHAFOLD_API_URL}{uniprot}?key={API_KEY}"

docs.title: alphafold_file_url(uniprot: UniProtId, model: int, file: str) -> AlphaFoldUrl
docs.text:
Create AlphaFold structure file URLs

Source code in affetch/main.py 
def alphafold_file_url(uniprot: UniProtId, model: int, file: str) -> AlphaFoldUrl:
    """ Create AlphaFold structure file URLs """
    return f"{ALPHAFOLD_FILE_URL}AF-{uniprot}-F1-model_v{model}.{file}"

docs.title: alphfold_file_url(uniprot: UniProtId, model: int, file: str) -> AlphaFoldUrl
docs.text:
Backward-compatible alias for the historical misspelling.

Source code in affetch/main.py 
def alphfold_file_url(uniprot: UniProtId, model: int, file: str) -> AlphaFoldUrl:
    """Backward-compatible alias for the historical misspelling."""
    return alphafold_file_url(uniprot, model, file)

docs.title: chunk_urls(urls: list[AlphaFoldUrl], n: int) -> Generator[list[AlphaFoldUrl], None, None]
docs.text:
Split a list of urls into chunks of size N

Source code in affetch/main.py 
def chunk_urls(urls: list[AlphaFoldUrl], n: int) -> Generator[list[AlphaFoldUrl], None, None]:
    """ Split a list of urls into chunks of size N """
    for i in range(0, len(urls), n):
        yield urls[i : i + n]

docs.title: parse_uniprot(uniprot: str) -> UniProtId
docs.text:
Parse UniProt IDs with regular expressions

Source code in affetch/main.py 
def parse_uniprot(uniprot: str) -> UniProtId:
    """ Parse UniProt IDs with regular expressions """
    uniprot_match = re.search(UNIPROT_RE, uniprot)
    if uniprot_match:
        return uniprot_match.group()
    return ""

docs.title: validate_fasta(fasta: str) -> list[UniProtId]
docs.text:
Read a FASTA file and return only valid UniProt IDs

Source code in affetch/main.py 
def validate_fasta(fasta: str) -> list[UniProtId]:
    """ Read a FASTA file and return only valid UniProt IDs """
    uniprot_ids = []
    with open(Path(fasta), encoding="utf-8") as open_fasta:
        for line in open_fasta:
            if line.startswith(">"):
                uniprot_id = parse_uniprot(line)
                if validate_uniprot_id(uniprot_id):
                    uniprot_ids.append(uniprot_id)

    return uniprot_ids

docs.title: validate_uniprot_id(uniprot: str) -> bool
docs.text:
Validate UniProt IDs with regular expressions

Source code in affetch/main.py 
def validate_uniprot_id(uniprot: str) -> bool:
    """ Validate UniProt IDs with regular expressions """
    return re.fullmatch(UNIPROT_RE, uniprot) is not None

docs.title: write_gz_structure(file_path: Path, structure: str) -> None
docs.text:
Source code in affetch/main.py 
def write_gz_structure(file_path: Path, structure: str) -> None:
    file_path = Path(f"{file_path}.gz")
    with gzip.open(file_path, "wb") as f:
        f.write(structure.encode("utf-8"))

docs.title: write_structure(file_path: Path, structure: str) -> None
docs.text:
Source code in affetch/main.py 
def write_structure(file_path: Path, structure: str) -> None:
    with open(file_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        f.write(structure)
